compilers.python: 3.10.12 (GIL enabled; no free-threaded build available on this machine)

machine.notes: All numbers are medians of interactive runs on a shared multi-tenant host; treat them as order-of-magnitude, not microarchitectural truth.

creation_cost.method: N=2000-5000 create+join (or fork+wait) pairs, CLOCK_MONOTONIC, warmup excluded

context_switch.method: two processes pinned to one core, 1-byte ping-pong over two pipes, 199k round trips; each round trip forces two switches

data_race_lost_updates.method: volatile long counter++ from 4 threads, gcc -O2; volatile forces the load/add/store to memory each iteration but provides no atomicity

counter_throughput_Mops.note: thread-local counters stay in registers under -O2, so that row measures the cost of sharing, not of addition

false_sharing_Mops.method: each thread increments its OWN atomic counter with relaxed ordering; packed = counters 8 bytes apart, padded = alignas(64)

cpu_bound.task: sum of i*i for i in range(2_000_000), pure Python, 8 tasks

python_gil.free_threaded: unavailable: this machine has CPython 3.10 only; the free-threaded comparison requires a 3.13+ build configured with --disable-gil
